title: NeuralPLexer3: Accurate Biomolecular Complex Structure Prediction with Flow Models
task: generalized biomolecular complex structure prediction; protein-ligand complex structure prediction; biomolecular interaction structure prediction; ligand-induced conformational change prediction; confidence estimation for predicted biomolecular complexes
datasets: PoseBusters-V2; NPBench; recent PDB bioassemblies / low-homology post-2023 PDB evaluation set; CASP15 RNA targets; ConfBench; PLINDER; OpenProteinSet; OrbNet conformers
models_or_methods: NeuralPLexer3 / NP3; physics-inspired flow-based generative model; conditional flow matching; continuous normalizing flow; flow-based encoder-decoder structure prediction model; informative globular polymer prior; Langevin dynamics prior relaxation; optimal transport structure permutation; Flash-TriangularAttention; confidence module; pLDDT/pDockQ-based sample ranking
metrics: RMSD < 2 Å success rate; ligand RMSD; PB-valid success rate / PoseBusters validity; ligand stereochemistry accuracy; LDDT; DockQ > 0.23 success rate; TM-score; pLDDT; pDockQ; ConfBench score; pocket_fident; bb_LDDT; inference time / prediction speed
review_notes: Broader than ligand docking: covers proteins, nucleic acids, ligands, ions, PTMs and multiple interaction types. PLINDER is mainly a curation source for ConfBench; OpenProteinSet and OrbNet conformers are training/augmentation datasets rather than evaluation benchmarks.

title: FlowDock: Geometric Flow Matching for Generative Protein-Ligand Docking and Affinity Prediction
task: flexible protein-ligand docking; protein-ligand complex structure prediction; protein-ligand binding affinity prediction; multi-ligand docking; apo-to-holo protein-ligand structure generation
datasets: PDBBind-E; PDBBind 2020; Binding MOAD; PoseBusters Benchmark / PoseBench PoseBusters set; DockGen-E; CASP16 ligand category; Astex Diverse subset; PLINDER
models_or_methods: FlowDock; geometric flow matching; conditional flow matching; deep geometric generative model; hybrid structure-and-affinity prediction model; apo-to-holo structure generation; harmonic ligand prior; ESMFold protein prior; fine-tuned NeuralPLexer architecture; generalized unbalanced flow matching; VD-ODE sampler; confidence head / pLDDT; binding affinity head
metrics: blind docking success rate; RMSD ≤ 2 Å and PB-Valid success rate; ligand RMSD; binding pocket conformation RMSD; pLDDT / structural confidence; Pearson correlation; Spearman correlation; RMSE; MAE; CASP16 affinity-ranking position; runtime; CPU memory usage; GPU memory usage; TM-score and RMSD apo-to-holo training filters
review_notes: Do not include centroid distance unless a later version explicitly reports it; it is not visible in this PDF. CASP16 is best treated as an external evaluation track/benchmark, not an ordinary training dataset. PLINDER appears as preliminary fine-tuning data, not a main evaluation set.

title: Harmonic Self-Conditioned Flow Matching for joint Multi-Ligand Docking and Binding Site Design
task: multi-ligand docking; pocket-level protein-ligand docking; 3D protein-ligand binding structure generation; binding site design; protein pocket residue design for small-molecule binding; joint residue-type and ligand-pose generation
datasets: PDBBind version 2020; PDBBind time split; PDBBind sequence similarity split; Binding MOAD; Binding MOAD sequence similarity split
models_or_methods: HARMONICFLOW; FLOWSITE; harmonic self-conditioned flow matching; self-conditioned flow matching; harmonic prior; equivariant refinement TFN layers; joint discrete-continuous flow; fake-ligand data augmentation; invariant graph attention network; product-space diffusion baseline; EigenFold diffusion baseline
metrics: RMSD; fraction of predictions with RMSD < 2 Å; fraction of predictions with RMSD < 5 Å; median RMSD; sequence recovery; BLOSUM score; runtime
review_notes: Product-space diffusion and EigenFold diffusion are central baselines for structure generation. ProteinMPNN, PiFold, random ligand position, DiffDock-Pocket pose and ground-truth pose are central binding-site-recovery baselines, but can be moved to a separate baselines field if using an 8-field schema.

title: Composing Unbalanced Flows for Flexible Docking and Relaxation
task: flexible protein-ligand docking; pocket-based flexible docking; structure relaxation; protein flexibility modeling; apo-to-holo conformational change modeling; generation of energetically favorable / physically valid docking poses
datasets: PDBBind; PDBBind ESMFold docking benchmark; PoseBusters v2 / PoseBusters benchmark
models_or_methods: Unbalanced Flow Matching; Flow Matching; FLEXDOCK; manifold docking flow / manifold Unbalanced FM; Euclidean relaxation flow / structure relaxation Unbalanced FM; confidence discriminator; energy-based loss; flat-bottom potential; energy filtering; SE(3) and side-chain torsion flow
metrics: ligand RMSD; median ligand RMSD; percentage of ligand RMSD < 2 Å; pocket all-atom RMSD / AA-RMSD; percentage of all-atom RMSD < 1 Å; PoseBusters checks; PB-valid; PB-valid and ligand RMSD < 2 Å; runtime; proportion of energetically favorable poses
review_notes: DiffDock-Pocket is the most methodologically central baseline. SMINA, GNINA, RE-DOCK, DeepDock, UniMol, GOLD, VINA, UMOL and AlphaFold3 are comparison baselines; move them to a baselines field if available.

title: ForceFM: Enhancing Protein-Ligand Predictions through Force-Guided Flow Matching
task: protein-ligand docking; molecular docking; binding pose prediction; physically plausible ligand conformation generation; low-energy ligand pose generation; blind self-docking; cross-domain docking generalization evaluation
datasets: PDBBind; PDBBind time split; PoseBusters Benchmark; DockGen test set / DockGen cross-domain benchmark
models_or_methods: ForceFM; force-guided flow matching; force-guidance network / force model; manifold flow matching; Riemannian flow matching; energy-guided generation; Vina-guided generation; Glide-guided generation; Gnina-guided generation; Confscore/Conf-guided generation; RDKit ETKDG conformer initialization
metrics: ligand RMSD; mean RMSD; RMSD ≤ 2 Å success rate; RMSD ≤ 5 Å success rate; RMSD percentiles; centroid distance; mean centroid distance; centroid distance ≤ 2 Å; centroid distance ≤ 5 Å; PoseBusters pass rate / physical validity; average runtime; inference cost; number of sampling steps / convergence speed
review_notes: Vina, Glide, Gnina and Confscore are better annotated as guidance/scoring functions under models_or_methods, not as evaluation metrics. No explicit docking-score metric was found in the PDF, so it is not included as a gold metric.
